title: carex vulpina subsp. glomerata
intro: Carex vulpina subsp. glomerata, also known as the fox sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Carex vulpina subsp. glomerata include its ability to aid in the healing of cuts and scrapes, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or salves, which can be used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
